item.title: “Heated Rivalry” and Its Wine-Mom Fans Reunite
item.description: Plus: the radiant pop of MUNA, the visceral paintings of Juanita McNeely, a “Beaches” musical, and more.

item.title: Rachel Syme Gets Suited Up
item.description: Also: Jessica Winter’s mom-themed movie picks
item.dc:creator: ByRachel SymeandJessica Winter

item.title: Two Hundred and Fifty Years of Complicated Commemorations
item.description: Donald Trump’s aversion to admitting fault suggests that we will not likely see events that grapple with the nuanced nature of the nation’s history this July 4th.
item.dc:creator: ByJelani Cobb

item.title: Colbert’s Trumpet Player on Life After Late Night
item.description: Since 2015, Jon Lampley has played in the house band of “The Late Show,” which CBS unceremoniously cancelled. As the final episode looms, he takes a look back.
item.dc:creator: ByMark Yarm

item.title: Is the Twenty-fifth AmendmentReallyan Option?
item.description: After J.F.K.’s assassination, a neophyte lawyer named John Feerick was summoned to Washington to draft the provision. Now everyone wants him to weigh in on booting Trump from office.
item.dc:creator: ByDiego Lasarte

item.title: On the High Line, Buddha Is the New Giant Pigeon
item.description: After the bird sculpture flew the Chelsea coop, the curator Cecilia Alemani oversaw the installation of a new work—a sacred sandstone colossus, based on one destroyed by the Taliban.
item.dc:creator: ByJulian Lucas

item.title: In HBO’s “The Dark Wizard,” Dean Potter Climbs On
item.description: TheBASEjumper died in a tragic accident in 2015, the day before he was to accept an award for performance art. A new docuseries explores his life and legacy.
item.dc:creator: ByNick Paumgarten

item.title: Was the Declaration of Independence Better Before the Edits?
item.description: Amid contention, criticism, and compromise, a divided nation had to present a unified front. It came at a cost.
item.dc:creator: ByJill Lepore

item.title: How Americans Caught Gold Fever Again
item.description: Soaring gold prices, viral panning influencers, macho gold-mining reality shows, and Trump’s gold obsession have ignited a craze for prospecting not seen since 1849.
item.dc:creator: ByJennifer Wilson

item.title: Barack Obama Considers His Role in the Age of Trump
item.description: The former President remains one of the most popular politicians in the country. What are his obligations to it?
item.dc:creator: ByPeter Slevin

item.title: The Life and Times of an American Tween
item.description: In some ways, the world is cooked. But being a twelve-year-old still kind of eats.
item.dc:creator: ByAnna Wiener

item.title: The Very American, Very Intense World of High-School Debate
item.description: “When it goes well, it can be extremely addicting.”
item.dc:creator: Photography byEli DurstText byJay Caspian Kang

item.title: Senior-Discount Disneyland
item.description: Get your coupons ready. Here comes Mr. Toad’s Wild Ride to the Urologist and the Tunnel of Nap.
item.dc:creator: ByIan Frazier

item.title: Mapping Trump’s America
item.description: A totally nonpartisan cartographic reinterpretation of the United States.
item.dc:creator: ByLiana Finck

item.title: “Standings”
item.description: We were mostly boys and young enough that the bonds felt fierce, like in a tribe, tourniquet tight, yet our tribe was fuelled by constant disagreements.
item.dc:creator: ByChang-rae Lee

item.title: The American Revolution Wasn’t the Main Event
item.description: Americans have long imagined that they set off a global age of revolt. Seen within the era’s wider wars of empire, the story looks rather different.
item.dc:creator: ByDaniel Immerwahr

item.title: The Idea That Reshaped Identity Politics Has a Complicated Backstory
item.description: Kimberlé Crenshaw gave us the terms “intersectionality” and “critical race theory.” Her new memoir shows that she isn’t done fighting over what they mean.
item.dc:creator: ByKelefa Sanneh

item.title: Briefly Noted
item.description: “Korean Messiah,” “Small Town Girls,” “Underlake,” and “August, September, October.”

item.title: The Artist Who Made America Look Like a Promised Land
item.description: Frederic Edwin Church sold a nation on its own mythology. That was his making—and his unmaking.
item.dc:creator: BySebastian Smee

item.title: Harriet Clark’s Début Is a New Kind of Coming-of-Age Novel
item.description: In “The Hill,” a daughter comes of age through visits to her imprisoned mother, inheriting the afterlife of a youthful radicalism that shattered her family.
item.dc:creator: ByJames Wood

item.title: “Schmigadoon!” and “The Lost Boys” Are Killer Revamps
item.description: Camp has become the go-to aesthetic for Broadway musicals. These two new shows dare to be sincere.
item.dc:creator: ByEmily Nussbaum

item.title: “The Devil Wears Prada 2” Gives the Decline of Magazines the Glossy Treatment
item.description: The sequel, which reunites Anne Hathaway, Meryl Streep, and Emily Blunt, is familiar and at times preposterous—but it’s also a savvy, shiny reflection of our era.
item.dc:creator: ByJustin Chang

item.title: “While My Daughter Is in Surgery I Think About a Night in a Hotel in Florence”
item.description: “She’d bought a black leather jacket from a stall on the Ponte Vecchio.”
item.dc:creator: ByEllen Bass

item.description: “Your hair is a yard long. Blue work shirt. Ripped jeans.”
item.dc:creator: ByBrenda Hillman

item.title: Cartoon Caption Contest
item.description: Submit a caption, plus vote on other entries and finalists.

item.title: Cartoons from the Issue
item.description: Drawings from the May 11 & 18, 2026, magazine.

item.title: The Crossword: Tuesday, April 28, 2026
item.description: Today’s theme: Homegrown.
item.dc:creator: ByAdam Wagner
